frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver grasping needle with suture attached, left instrument positioning
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle grasp position, clean instrument handling

frame_analyses.description: Needle being positioned for entry through Penrose drain at marked location
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Approaching first mark, good needle angle

frame_analyses.description: Needle passed through drain, working toward second mark
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean needle passage, suture following appropriately

frame_analyses.description: Beginning knot tying sequence, forming first throw (surgeon's knot double throw)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Right hand leading the throw, good loop formation

frame_analyses.description: Completing first throw with double wrap, cinching down
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean surgeon's knot formation, appropriate tension

frame_analyses.description: Forming second throw, left hand now leading
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good hand switching, single throw formation

frame_analyses.description: Final throw being formed, right hand leading again
Cutting suture tails with scissors inside trainer box
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both tails being cut intracorporeally as required

penalties.description: Entry point appears approximately 1-2mm from mark 1

knot_assessments.notes: Clean double throw formation with appropriate tension
Proper hand switching maintained, good throw quality
Final throw completed with proper alternating sequence

confidence_rationale: Clear view of knot tying sequence and hand switching. Suture placement deviation estimated from available camera angles. Task completion and timing well-documented.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter measurements at suture entry points due to camera angle limitations
technique_summary: Excellent intermediate to expert-level performance with proper knot sequence, consistent hand switching, and efficient timing. Minimal penalties for slight suture placement deviation.
strengths: Excellent knot tying technique with proper surgeon's knot formation
Consistent hand switching between all throws as required
Efficient overall timing at 120 seconds (intermediate range)
improvement_suggestions: Focus on more precise needle placement at marked locations to eliminate deviation penalties
Consider reducing suture placement time from 27s to under 20s for expert-level performance
